metadata.description: Comprehensive remote sensing satellite constellation dataset for webapp integration
sources: Wikipedia List of Earth Observation Satellites
metadata.notes: Operational status, satellite counts, and specs reflect best available data as of June 2026. Some specs (especially commercial tasking) are approximate or subject to NDA. 'open' access = freely available without cost. 'commercial' = paid licensing. 'restricted' = government/defense only or requires proposal approval.

cadence_definitions.subdaily: Multiple passes per day over a given location

constellations.name: Landsat 8/9
constellations.description: Cornerstone of the 50-year US land monitoring archive. OLI + TIRS instruments. Free and open global archive.

constellations.name: Sentinel-2
constellations.description: EU's flagship optical multispectral constellation. S-2A, S-2B, S-2C operational. Free and open. 5-day revisit with 2 satellites.

constellations.name: Sentinel-1
constellations.description: Free C-band SAR. S-1A, S-1C, S-1D operational (S-1B retired 2021). All-weather day/night. Widely used for InSAR, floods, sea ice.

constellations.name: Sentinel-3
constellations.description: Ocean & land colour, sea surface temperature, sea level altimetry. OLCI (21 bands) + SLSTR instruments.

constellations.name: Sentinel-5 Precursor (TROPOMI)
constellations.description: Atmospheric composition: NO2, SO2, CO, O3, CH4, aerosols. TROPOMI instrument, daily global coverage at 3.5 × 5.5 km.

constellations.name: Sentinel-6 (Jason-CS)
constellations.description: Sea surface height altimetry continuing Jason series. Poseidon-4 dual-frequency radar altimeter. Named Michael Freilich.

constellations.name: PlanetScope (SuperDove)
constellations.description: World's largest imaging constellation. 200+ Dove/SuperDove CubeSats. Daily global land coverage. 8-band VNIR.

constellations.name: SkySat
constellations.description: High-resolution tasking. 15 satellites, up to 10× daily revisit. 50 cm panchromatic. Being phased out in favour of Pelican.

constellations.name: Pelican
constellations.description: Next-gen replacement for SkySat. Up to 30 cm resolution, 30× daily revisit at scale. Gen-2 includes on-orbit NVIDIA AI processing. 32-satellite target constellation.

constellations.name: Tanager (Planet)
constellations.description: Methane and CO2 point-source detection. Tanager-1 launched Aug 2024 via Carbon Mapper Coalition. ~300,000 km²/day coverage.

constellations.name: WorldView-1/2/3 (Vantor)
constellations.description: Legacy very-high-resolution optical fleet. WV-3 at 31 cm pan, 1.24 m MS + SWIR. Being succeeded by WorldView Legion.

constellations.name: WorldView Legion
constellations.description: 6-satellite 30 cm constellation, all launched 2024–Feb 2025. Up to 15× daily revisit. 6.6 million km²/day capacity. Precision geolocation <5 m CE90.

constellations.name: Pléiades Neo
constellations.description: European 30 cm VHR optical constellation. 4 identical satellites. Intra-day revisit anywhere on Earth. Two lost on Vega-C (Pléiades Neo 5/6, 2022 launch failure).

constellations.name: SPOT-6/7
constellations.description: 1.5 m pan resolution wide-area tasking. Complementary to Pléiades for large-area coverage at lower resolution.

constellations.name: ICEYE SAR Constellation
constellations.description: World's largest commercial SAR constellation with 62 satellites as of late 2025. Gen4 delivers 16–25 cm spotlight resolution, 400 km high-res field of regard. Sub-daily revisit. Also supports sovereign national programs (Greece, Poland).

constellations.name: Capella Space
constellations.description: US X-band SAR constellation with some of the highest commercial SAR resolution (35 cm spotlight). Whitney-generation satellites operational. NRO commercial vendor.

constellations.name: Umbra SAR
constellations.description: Claims highest-resolution commercial SAR imagery at 25 cm spotlight. 11 satellites as of Nov 2025. Open data program for research. NRO commercial vendor. Partnered with Vantor (Maxar).

constellations.name: COSMO-SkyMed (1st + 2nd Gen)
constellations.description: 4 first-generation + 2 second-generation X-band SAR satellites. Spotlight mode <1 m. Dual civil/military use. Research access via ESA proposal. Second-gen has 300% bandwidth increase, finer resolution.

constellations.name: TerraSAR-X / TanDEM-X / PAZ
constellations.description: TerraSAR-X + TanDEM-X fly in close formation enabling global DEM. PAZ (Spain) shares same orbit. SpotLight mode: 1 × 1.7 m. Commercial access via Airbus.

constellations.name: RADARSAT-2 / RCM
constellations.description: RADARSAT-2 (single sat, 2007) + RCM (3-satellite constellation, 2019). C-band SAR with compact polarimetry. Designed for maritime surveillance, disaster monitoring, Arctic monitoring.

constellations.name: SAOCOM
constellations.description: Argentina's L-band SAR constellation. Penetrates vegetation and soil. Particularly useful for soil moisture, agricultural monitoring. Data policy partly open.

constellations.name: ALOS-2 / ALOS-4
constellations.description: JAXA's L-band SAR. ALOS-2 PALSAR-2 instrument. ALOS-4 (2024) adds improved PALSAR-3. L-band penetrates through vegetation for biomass mapping, crop monitoring, subsidence.

constellations.name: MODIS (Terra / Aqua)
constellations.description: Workhorse of climate and environmental science. 36 bands, daily global coverage at 250–1000 m. Terra (1999) + Aqua (2002). Near end of mission but still operational.

constellations.name: VIIRS (NPP / NOAA-20 / NOAA-21)
constellations.description: JPSS program. Successor to MODIS. 375 m day/night band plus 750 m imagery band. 22 spectral bands. 3 satellites currently operational providing daily global coverage.

constellations.name: GOES-16/17/18 (GOES-R series)
constellations.description: Geostationary weather and environment monitoring. Full-disk image every 10 min, CONUS every 5 min. ABI instrument. GOES-U (2024) also now operational.

constellations.name: Meteosat / MTG
constellations.description: European geostationary weather constellation. MSG series + MTG-I1 (launched 2022). MTG brings 16 FCI channels and lightning imager. Covers Europe, Africa, Middle East.

constellations.name: Himawari-8/9
constellations.description: Japanese GEO weather. 16-band AHI instrument comparable to GOES-R ABI. Full-disk every 10 min, Japan rapid scan every 2.5 min.

constellations.name: Jilin-1
constellations.description: China's largest commercial EO constellation. 117+ satellites as of 2024 (target: 300). Sub-metre resolution optical + video. Also includes hyperspectral variants (Jilin-1 GF series).

constellations.name: Satellogic (Aleph-1 / ÑuSat)
constellations.description: 50+ NewSat satellites. Sub-metre panchromatic. Each satellite also collects hyperspectral video. Partnered with Vantor (Maxar) for joint tasking.

constellations.name: KOMPSAT Series
constellations.description: Korean Multi-Purpose Satellites. KOMPSAT-2, 3, 3A (55 cm optical), 5 (SAR). Distributed by SI Imaging Services (SIIS). Also includes KOMPSAT-5 X-band SAR.

constellations.name: GeoEye-1
constellations.description: Legacy 41 cm pan resolution imagery. Still operational, part of Vantor constellation. Also archived as WorldView fleet complement.

constellations.name: Pixxel Firefly
constellations.description: World's highest-resolution commercial hyperspectral constellation. All 6 Firefly satellites launched Jan 2025 on Transporter-12. 135 bands, up to 45 selectable per capture. Next phase: Honeybee (SWIR, 5 m, ~450 bands).

constellations.name: GHGSat
constellations.description: Dedicated methane and CO2 point-source monitoring. 13 satellites as of June 2025, world's largest dedicated GHG fleet. 25 m resolution. Observed 4M+ facilities in 2024.

constellations.name: ICEYE Sovereign Programs
constellations.description: National SAR programs built on ICEYE platform: Finnish, Greek (national program), Polish MikroSAR, BAE Systems Azalea, and others. Government-restricted access.

constellations.name: Fengyun Series
constellations.description: China's national weather satellite program. GEO (FY-2, FY-4 series) + polar (FY-3 series). FY-4A/B operational GEO. FY-3 D/E/F operational LEO. International data sharing.

constellations.name: Gaofen Series
constellations.spectral_range: Pan + MS; also SAR (GF-3) and hyperspectral (GF-5)
constellations.description: China's high-resolution national civil/military EO program. GF-1 through GF-7 cover optical, SAR, stereo, hyperspectral. GF-7 at 65 cm stereo. Limited international access; some via ESA.

constellations.name: ResourceSat / Cartosat (ISRO)
constellations.description: India's land mapping and resources constellation. Cartosat-3 at 25 cm pan (world-class), ResourceSat for agricultural monitoring. Distributed via NRSC.

constellations.name: PAZ
constellations.description: Spanish X-band SAR in the same orbit as TerraSAR-X/TanDEM-X trio. Spanish national security + commercial use. Airbus commercial distribution.

constellations.name: NovaSAR-S
constellations.description: UK S-band SAR microsatellite. First commercially procured spaceborne S-band SAR. Wide-swath maritime surveillance. Very affordable data.

constellations.name: Synspective StriX
constellations.description: Japanese commercial X-band SAR constellation. StriX-α, β, 1, 2, 3, 4 operational. Target 30-satellite constellation for hourly revisit.

constellations.name: EarthCARE
constellations.spectral_range: ATLID lidar (355 nm) + MSI + BBR + CPR
constellations.description: Joint ESA/JAXA cloud and aerosol profiling mission launched May 2024. 4 instruments: ATLID lidar, MSI radiometer, BBR broadband, CPR cloud profiling radar.

constellations.name: PACE (NASA)
constellations.spectral_range: UV-NIR OCI: 340–890 nm; HARP2 + SPEXone polarimeters
constellations.description: NASA ocean colour, aerosol, cloud ecosystem. OCI instrument: 200-band hyperspectral ocean colour. Also carries HARP2 and SPEXone multi-angle polarimeters. Launched Feb 2024.

constellations.name: Biomass (ESA)
constellations.description: ESA Earth Explorer. First space P-band SAR. Measures global forest biomass and carbon. Penetrates forest canopy. Launched 2025.

constellations.name: GRUS (Axelspace)
constellations.description: Japanese commercial 2.5 m constellation. 5 GRUS-1 satellites. AxelGlobe platform providing frequent medium-resolution monitoring.

constellations.name: Disaster Monitoring Constellation (DMC)
constellations.description: Multi-nation constellation for rapid disaster response. Includes NigeriaSat-2, Deimos-2 (Spain), UK-DMC3, Beijing-1. Very wide swath for rapid area coverage.

constellations.name: ICESat-2
constellations.description: NASA photon-counting lidar for ice sheet height, sea ice thickness, vegetation canopy height, ocean bathymetry. 91-day exact repeat. ATLAS instrument.

constellations.name: GRACE-FO
constellations.description: Twin satellites measuring Earth's gravity field changes. Key for groundwater depletion, ice mass loss, sea level. Monthly global gravity maps. Laser Ranging Interferometer as tech demo.

constellations.name: SWOT
constellations.description: Surface Water and Ocean Topography. Wide-swath Ka-band radar altimeter (KaRIn). Maps all water bodies >250 m wide. First mission to measure global surface water storage.

constellations.name: BlackSky (NTT DATA)
constellations.description: 16 Gen-3 satellites. Sub-metre optical with rapid revisit focus (~6× daily). Acquired by NTT DATA. Specialises in real-time analytics platform and monitoring.

constellations.name: BRO (Unseenlabs)
constellations.description: RF/spectrum monitoring nanosatellites for maritime surveillance. Detects and geolocates vessel AIS transmissions and non-cooperative emitters. 15 BRO satellites.

constellations.name: CO3D
constellations.description: Constellation of Optical Stereo Satellites for a 3D Digital Surface Model of the entire Earth. 4 satellites collecting stereo pairs. Primary product: global 50 cm DSM. Launched 2025.

constellations.name: PRISMA
constellations.description: Italian Space Agency hyperspectral mission. 237 bands from 400–2500 nm at 30 m. Pan at 5 m. Free for research via ASI. Key dataset for agriculture, geology, environmental monitoring.

constellations.name: DESIS (Teledyne-FLIR / DLR)
constellations.description: VNIR hyperspectral instrument on ISS MUSES platform. 235 bands, 30 m resolution. Non-sun-synchronous coverage due to ISS orbit. Non-polar coverage only.

constellations.name: RapidEye (archive)
constellations.description: Decommissioned 2020. First commercial constellation to include a red-edge band. Large archive available. Influential for vegetation monitoring and agriculture.

constellations.name: Landsat 5 (TM)
constellations.description: Longest-lived Landsat; operated 1984–2013, anchors the 30 m continuous record. Thematic Mapper (TM) instrument.

constellations.name: Landsat 7 (ETM+)
constellations.description: Scan-line corrector (SLC) failed 2003, producing striped data gaps. Operated until 2022; large archive available.

constellations.name: DMSP-OLS (Nightlights)
constellations.description: Defense Meteorological Satellite Program nighttime lights archive. The Henderson–Storeygard–Weil luminosity lineage. 6-bit sensor, saturates over bright cities.

constellations.name: NISAR
constellations.description: First dual-frequency L+S band SAR satellite; measures deformation, biomass, ice dynamics. Joint NASA–ISRO mission. Launched July 2025.

constellations.name: GEDI
constellations.description: Global Ecosystem Dynamics Investigation. Spaceborne lidar measuring canopy height, 3D structure, and biomass. Sampling footprints (not wall-to-wall), 25 m diameter shots.

constellations.name: SMAP
constellations.description: Soil Moisture Active Passive. Measures global surface soil moisture (9–36 km) and freeze/thaw state every 2–3 days. Radar arm failed shortly after launch; radiometer operational.

constellations.name: GPM Core Observatory
constellations.description: Global Precipitation Measurement Core Observatory. Dual-frequency precipitation radar + GMI microwave imager. Anchors the GPM constellation of partner satellites.

constellations.name: EMIT
constellations.description: Earth Surface Mineral Dust Source Investigation. VSWIR imaging spectrometer on ISS. Maps surface mineralogy; also used to detect methane and CO2 point sources.

constellations.name: EnMAP
constellations.description: German environmental mapping hyperspectral mission. 228 bands at 30 m. On-demand tasking. Free for research via DLR. Target areas re-visited on 27-day cycle when tasked.

constellations.name: COSMIC-2
constellations.description: Constellation Observing System for Meteorology, Ionosphere, and Climate – 2. 6 satellites providing GNSS radio occultation atmospheric profiles for weather forecasting and climate.

constellations.name: ECOSTRESS
constellations.description: ECOsystem Spaceborne Thermal Radiometer on the ISS. Measures plant temperature, evapotranspiration, and water use. 70 m resolution thermal infrared sampling.

constellations.name: Pléiades 1A/1B
constellations.description: Twin 50 cm VHR optical constellation. Predecessor to Pléiades Neo. Daily worldwide tasking capability. Phasing out as Neo handles primary workload.

constellations.name: BlackSky Gen-2
constellations.description: AI-analytics-first monitoring constellation at 1 m resolution. Gen-2 workhorse satellites; ~6× daily sub-metre revisit over target areas.

constellations.name: BlackSky Gen-3
constellations.description: Next-generation 35 cm sub-hourly revisit. Targeting 8–9 satellites on orbit by end 2026. Significantly improved resolution over Gen-2.

constellations.name: Wyvern
constellations.description: Canadian commercial hyperspectral constellation using diffractive optics. On-demand tasking. First commercial hyperspectral operator to launch in 2023.
